name: Metric Selection Review
description: Review candidate metrics for an ML task and produce a justified evaluation metric plan without running evaluations.

inputs.description: Task, capability, risk, or benchmark target that needs metric selection.
Optional candidate metrics, measurements, or benchmark scores to compare.
Dataset, benchmark, or sample set that the metric plan should account for.

permissions.description: Read public evaluation docs, dataset cards, model cards, papers, and benchmark references.

approval_points.description: Confirm the selected metrics, tradeoffs, thresholds, and known blind spots.

phases.name: Objective
phases.objective: Define the evaluation question, intended users, failure modes, and acceptance criteria.

phases.name: Candidate Review
phases.objective: Compare candidate metrics against task fit, data availability, interpretability, and failure modes.

phases.name: Metric Spec
phases.objective: Document selected metrics, aggregation, thresholds, split handling, tie-breakers, and reporting format.

phases.name: Risk Review
phases.objective: Identify metric gaming, reliability risks, sample-level debugging needs, and human-review checkpoints.

required_outputs.description: Evaluation objective, users, failure modes, and acceptance criteria.
Candidate metrics with sources, assumptions, strengths, and weaknesses.
Selected metrics, aggregation, thresholds, split handling, and reporting format.
Reliability, gaming, interpretation, and debugging risks.

artifacts.description: Decision record for chosen and rejected metrics.
Metric references, benchmark docs, papers, and card links.

verifiers.description: Evaluation objective maps to observable examples, comparisons, and metrics.
Metric choices and benchmark assumptions include source links and source types.
Metrics, comparisons, measurements, aggregation, and thresholds are documented.
Reliability risks, metric failure modes, and sample-level debugging needs were reviewed.
Final recommendation cites metric sources, tradeoff analysis, and risk notes.
